relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Simeon's lot falls inside Judah's earlier inheritance.
Beer-sheba, Sheba, and Moladah begin Simeon's towns.
Hazar-shual, Balah, and Ezem follow.
Eltolad, Bethul, and Hormah are named.
Ziklag, Beth-marcaboth, and Hazar-susah are listed.
Beth-lebaoth and Sharuhen close the first group.
Ain, Rimmon, Ether, and Ashan complete another cluster.
Villages as far as Baalath-beer finish Simeon's share.
Judah's oversized portion is reduced to make room for Simeon.
Zebulun's lot rises next, beginning near Sarid.
Its border moves through Maralah, Dabbesheth, and Jokneam's wadi.
Chisloth-tabor, Daberath, and Japhia carry the eastern line.
Gath-hepher, Eth-kazin, Rimmon, and Neah shape the next turn.
Hannathon and Iphtah-el close the border.
Kattath, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem are named among twelve cities.
Zebulun's inheritance closes by clan.
Issachar receives the fourth lot.
Jezreel, Chesulloth, and Shunem begin its territory.
Hapharaim, Shion, and Anaharath follow.
Rabbith, Kishion, and Ebez are listed.
Remeth, En-gannim, En-haddah, and Beth-pazzez continue the register.
Tabor, Shahazumah, Beth-shemesh, and Jordan close sixteen cities.
Issachar's inheritance is sealed by clans.
Asher receives the fifth lot.
Helkath, Hali, Beten, and Achshaph begin the list.
Carmel and Shihor-libnath shape the western reach.
Beth-dagon, Zebulun, Iphtah-el, Beth-emek, Neiel, and Cabul mark the turns.
Ebron, Rehob, Hammon, Kanah, and Sidon expand the horizon.
Ramah, Tyre, Hosah, the sea, and Achzib complete the border.
Ummah, Aphek, and Rehob close twenty-two cities.
Asher's inheritance is named by clans.
Naphtali receives the sixth lot.
Its border begins at Heleph and moves through Zaanannim, Adami-nekeb, Jabneel, Lakkum, and Jordan.
Aznoth-tabor and Hukkok connect Naphtali to Zebulun, Asher, and Jordan.
Ziddim, Zer, Hammath, Rakkath, and Chinnereth are fortified cities.
Adamah, Ramah, and Hazor are listed.
Kedesh, Edrei, and En-hazor follow.
Iron, Migdal-el, Horem, Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh close nineteen cities.
Naphtali's inheritance is sealed by clans.
Dan receives the seventh lot.
Zorah, Eshtaol, and Ir-shemesh begin Dan's territory.
Shaalabbin, Aijalon, and Ithlah follow.
Elon, Timnah, and Ekron are named.
Eltekeh, Gibbethon, and Baalath follow.
Jehud, Bene-berak, and Gath-rimmon are listed.
Me-jarkon, Rakkon, and the Joppa border finish the assigned territory.
Dan's territory proves insufficient, and Leshem is captured, struck, possessed, and renamed.
Dan's inheritance closes with cities and villages.
Only after the tribes are served does Joshua receive an inheritance among them.
He asks for Timnath-serah, builds it, and dwells there.
At Shiloh, priest, Joshua, and tribal heads complete the division before the tent.

contemplative_note: Joshua 19 turns many tribes into many maps, but its two most human signals are adjustment and restraint: Judah's oversized share makes room for Simeon, and Joshua receives last. A just distribution should be flexible enough to correct excess and humble enough not to enrich leaders first.
